<findings>

[observation] [info] Cohesion of 0.63 is acceptable for a two-service zone; if a third usage-related service is added later, the zone boundary will strengthen naturally.
[observation] [info] Coupling (0.38) to web-dashboard-application via 2 mutual imports is appropriate for application-wired services; no action needed unless this grows significantly.
[observation] [info] The cleanup scheduler pattern (producer + scheduled purge) is a clean separation of write and maintenance concerns — a positive architectural choice.
[relationship] [info] Correctly isolated from all other satellite zones — depends only on web-dashboard-application. No action needed; this boundary should be preserved as new usage-related services are added.
[suggestion] [info] Normalize the file naming pair: rename 'usage-cleanup-scheduler.ts' to 'task-usage-cleanup.ts' to match the 'task-usage-' prefix of its partner 'incremental-task-usage.ts'. This makes both files discoverable under the same prefix and aligns with the domain-first naming convention used by the test files (incremental-task-usage.test.ts, usage-cleanup-scheduler.test.ts — the test for the scheduler also needs renaming).

</findings>

<insights>

- The two services form a natural producer/consumer pair: incremental-task-usage records data, usage-cleanup-scheduler purges it — keeping them co-located is correct
- Moderate coupling (0.38) reflects bidirectional imports with the main dashboard zone, which is expected for services that the server wires up at startup
- Each service ships with a dedicated unit test, indicating good test coverage discipline for these data-management concerns
- Cohesion of 0.63 is acceptable for a two-service zone; if a third usage-related service is added later, the zone boundary will strengthen naturally.
- The cleanup scheduler pattern (producer + scheduled purge) is a clean separation of write and maintenance concerns — a positive architectural choice.
- Coupling (0.38) to web-dashboard-application via 2 mutual imports is appropriate for application-wired services; no action needed unless this grows significantly.
- Zero lateral coupling to websocket-health-infrastructure and prd-analysis-input-panels confirms correct scope isolation — the services are wired together only at the hub, not at the spoke level
- Correctly isolated from all other satellite zones — depends only on web-dashboard-application. No action needed; this boundary should be preserved as new usage-related services are added.
- The two source files use asymmetric naming prefixes: 'incremental-task-usage.ts' leads with the data pattern ('incremental'), while 'usage-cleanup-scheduler.ts' leads with the domain ('usage'). A consistent convention would be either 'task-usage-recorder.ts' / 'task-usage-cleanup.ts' (domain-first) or 'incremental-task-usage.ts' / 'scheduled-usage-cleanup.ts' (pattern-first). The current asymmetry makes the pair harder to discover via prefix search.
- Normalize the file naming pair: rename 'usage-cleanup-scheduler.ts' to 'task-usage-cleanup.ts' to match the 'task-usage-' prefix of its partner 'incremental-task-usage.ts'. This makes both files discoverable under the same prefix and aligns with the domain-first naming convention used by the test files (incremental-task-usage.test.ts, usage-cleanup-scheduler.test.ts — the test for the scheduler also needs renaming).
- [call graph] 100 internal calls, 1 outgoing, 1 incoming (cohesion: 0.99, coupling: 0.01)

</insights>
